confirmations.reason: Project-Level AGENTS.md is exactly a repository-scoped instruction file that provides the agent with standing project-specific instructions and context, directly matching the capability.
Plugins explicitly bundle skills, app integrations, and MCP servers into reusable units that Codex can load, directly matching the 'bundle reusable capabilities/instructions' definition.
Hooks inject user scripts into Codex's agentic loop on lifecycle events (SessionStart, etc.), which is precisely running custom logic on agent lifecycle events.

AGENTS.md files provide persistent custom guidance/instructions but do not directly shape the system prompt, persona, or output style in the way the capability describes (prompt/persona customization). They are instruction files, not system-prompt or output-format controls.
Per-Agent Model Selection allows assigning specific models to each agent (auto or pinned), directly realizing the ability to choose which underlying model an agent runs on.
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is explicitly described as the standard protocol for connecting external tools and context providers, directly matching the capability.
MCP Server CLI Management provides CLI commands to add and manage MCP servers, directly covering install, configure, and management aspects of the capability.
OAuth MCP Login authenticates to MCP servers via OAuth, directly realizing MCP authentication.

MCP Tool Exposure describes that MCP servers expose callable tools for the model to use — this is about surfacing tools, not governing/allow-listing which tools the agent is permitted to use. Governance/allow-listing is a control mechanism, not mere exposure.
Memories is described as durable, cross-session memory of user preferences and project context, directly matching persistent agent memory across turns/sessions.
Compaction explicitly manages context window limits by compacting accumulated history in long-running conversations, directly realizing context compaction.
Container Caching caches configured containers to speed up task startup — this is infrastructure/environment caching, not prompt or context caching to cut latency/cost on repeated context.
Subagent Workflows describes Codex spawning specialized agents in parallel to handle parts of a task, directly matching the capability of delegating to sub-agents.
Multi-Agent Workflows describes coordinating multiple Codex agents via the Agents SDK and MCP server orchestration, directly realizing multi-agent teams that share work and communicate.

Automations run recurring tasks unattended on a schedule, which maps to scheduled/background execution rather than defining repeatable, named multi-step task sequences (saved workflow definitions). The feature is closer to scheduled-runs than to workflow authoring.

The build-test-run loop describes the agent autonomously iterating through build, test, and run phases until a goal condition (passing tests/successful run) is met, directly realizing goal-directed autonomous iteration.
Git Worktree Isolation runs each automation in an isolated worktree, enabling parallel work on multiple branches without interference, directly realizing parallel isolated workspaces.
Cloud Environments explicitly provides managed cloud execution with installable tooling, caching, secrets, and defaults — directly matching the capability.
Automations run tasks unattended in the background and report results, directly realizing background/async execution without blocking the user.
Standalone Automations are independent scheduled tasks that run on their own without a conversation thread, directly realizing scheduled/triggered runs without supervision.
Sandbox is explicitly a constrained environment that lets Codex run commands autonomously without unrestricted access, directly realizing sandboxed execution to limit blast radius.
Approval Policy controls when Codex must stop and ask before crossing sandbox boundaries, directly realizing tool permission controls (approve/deny what the agent may do).
Network Access Control enables or disables outbound network access with optional domain allow-listing, directly matching the capability of restricting the agent's outbound network access.

DNS Rebinding Protection prevents a specific network-layer attack (DNS rebinding) within the sandbox. While it is a security measure, it does not address prompt-injection (malicious instructions embedded in tool/web content). These are categorically different threats.
Codex Security is a security-review plugin that scans repositories for vulnerabilities and validates files, directly realizing automated security scanning against a threat model.

Universal Base Image is a pre-built default container image with common tools — it is a default runtime image, not dev-container integration (i.e., running the agent inside a user-defined devcontainer spec). It is adjacent but not the same capability.
Built-in Run Tracing emits a structured record of model calls, tool calls, handoffs, guardrails, and custom spans — directly realizing audit logging/telemetry of agent actions.
AI Code Review has Codex review GitHub pull request diffs and post structured code reviews focused on serious issues, directly realizing automated code review of diffs/PRs.
Evals API allows configuring and running evaluations of model outputs programmatically, directly realizing an evals/testing harness against agent behavior.
Multi-Device Session Handoff enables resuming an active Codex session from any signed-in device, directly realizing the resume/fork session capability.
Remote Connections lets users control an agent from another device or machine, including starting/continuing sessions, directly realizing remote control from mobile/web.
Task Completion Notifications deliver alerts when Codex finishes a task or needs input, directly realizing push notifications on run progress/completion.
Codex for Linear is a connector that lets users delegate work to Codex directly from Linear issues (by assigning or mentioning @Codex), directly realizing chat/issue-tool surface integration.

GitHub Integration is a specific integration for PR review and async cloud tasks — it is one particular access surface/connector, not a description of the full set of access surfaces (terminal, IDE, web, cloud, mobile). Mapping a single integration to the broad 'access surfaces' capability is too partial.
